Next: refresh 2sh.4 dependencies and acceptance instructions to match the current 3-bead plan.

title: Polish shell chrome styling for title, tabs, context, and footer
description:
## Description
Strengthen visual hierarchy so the shell header reads as product chrome rather than always-on help text.

## Instructions
1. Add or reuse styling helpers so the app title is emphasized, the active tab is highlighted, inactive tabs are dimmed, and right-side context/footer hints are visually muted.
2. Keep the style treatment readable in narrow widths and avoid making the footer louder than the board content.
3. Keep shell-specific styling localized to app/UI helpers rather than scattering raw Lip Gloss styling across unrelated call sites.
4. Update tests or goldens if the new styling changes expected rendering output.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/app/model.go` — apply shell chrome styles.
- `internal/ui/styles/colors.go` — add or reuse shell chrome color tokens.
- `internal/ui/styles/format.go` — add tiny formatting helpers if needed.
- `internal/app/model_test.go` — update expectations if rendering changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Active tab has a distinct highlighted treatment.
- [ ] Inactive tabs, right-side context, and footer hints render with lower-emphasis muted styling.
- [ ] The shell remains readable at narrow widths without reintroducing multiple help lines.
close_reason: Merged into beads-workbench-2sh.1 after reviewer found redundant boundary

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Dependency on task 1 is correct but the styling scope is redundant with task 1 work. What: task 1 must already render tabs with some visual treatment (the current code already applies foreground/background for active vs inactive tabs at model.go:585-591). Task 2 says 'add or reuse styling helpers so the app title is emphasized, the active tab is highlighted, inactive tabs are dimmed.' This is almost exactly what task 1 must implement to render the header correctly. Why: either task 1 leaves the header unstyled (which fails its own AC of rendering Board/Search tabs), or task 2 arrives and finds the styling is already done. This creates re-work or confusion. Suggested action: merge task 2 into task 1. The header chrome styling is not separable from the header layout — you cannot implement a single-line header with tabs without making tab styling decisions. If you want to keep them separate, task 1's instructions must explicitly say 'use placeholder/unstyled rendering' and task 2 must be very clear about what it changes vs what task 1 did.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acceptance criteria are not testable — 'distinct highlighted treatment' and 'lower-emphasis muted styling' are subjective. What: the criteria say active tab has 'distinct highlighted treatment' and inactive tabs render with 'lower-emphasis muted styling'. There is no way to verify this in a test except by checking specific ANSI escape codes, which the existing golden tests strip. Why: the verifier cannot pass or fail this without running the app and judging visually. Suggested action: either (a) define the specific styles (e.g., active tab = bold + white-on-blue, inactive = dimmed foreground, footer = TextMutedColor), or (b) state that acceptance is visual-only review by the user. Option (a) is better because it lets the tasker make the right choices without guessing.

title: Add stable process-level capture only where in-process verification is insufficient
description:
## Description
Add a narrow, explicit process-level capture path only for the cases that the primary in-process verification strategy cannot cover. This task exists to handle justified gaps, not to replace the faster deterministic default workflow.

## Scope
- Use the decision from gyg.1 to identify any concrete gap that still requires process-level capture
- If needed, implement a stable bounded capture path with explicit timeout/readiness/cleanup behavior
- Keep this path optional and focused on manual review or narrow automation cases
- Do not duplicate coverage already handled well by in-process embedded-fixture verification

## Files to Inspect
- findings from gyg.1
- runtime harness/spike code if any
- `docs/TESTING.md` and related workflow docs
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Any process-level capture added by this epic is justified by a concrete gap
- [ ] Process-level capture has explicit timeout, readiness, and cleanup behavior
- [ ] The process-level path is optional/narrow and does not replace the fast default workflow
- [ ] The chosen approach avoids known-fragile transcript-only capture
close_reason: Documented that no additional default process-level harness is justified now; process-level capture remains optional and tightly bounded by explicit readiness, timeout, and cleanup requirements.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Task .4 "fast entrypoints" is vague — what's the user story?

**What**: Task .4 says "provide fast commands or scripts" and "short commands for common scenarios." The acceptance criteria say "there are quick entrypoints for common UI verification scenarios" and "agents can run a basic layout/search/detail review in a small number of commands." This doesn't specify what form these take: Go test functions with `-run` patterns? Shell scripts? A `make` target? A custom CLI subcommand?

**Why**: The existing testing docs already provide fast entrypoints: `go test ./internal/app -run TestModelEmbeddedFixture... -v` runs the embedded-fixture smoke tests. The full-app verification workflow in TESTING.md is a 3-line shell sequence. If task .4 just produces another layer of aliases for these existing commands, it's not adding value.

**Suggested action**: Define the concrete form factor. The most useful version is probably a single `go test` invocation that runs all runtime UI verification scenarios with clear pass/fail output and readable failure diffs. If that's what this task produces, say so. If it's something else, explain why `go test -run` isn't sufficient.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Status: completed by explicit narrow decision.
Outcome: no new default process-level harness was added because current in-process embedded-fixture + teatest + assertion/golden coverage already covers the main runtime risk surface; built-binary full-app verification remains the manual process-level check.
Artifacts: docs/TESTING.md process-level capture policy section.
Verification: go test ./internal/testing/ui ./internal/app -run 'TestAssertionHelpersCoverStartupErrorsSearchAndActions|TestSearchModeReusableScenarioHelpersCoverTypingFragileAndClear|TestModelReusableBoardSearchDetailScenarioCoversTypingClearScrollAndBack|TestModelEmbeddedFixtureStartupLoadsBoardWithoutGatewaySectionErrors' -v\nNext step: document the full workflow clearly in gyg.5 and verify the final workflow in gyg.6.

title: Document runtime UI verification workflow in testing docs
description:
## Description
Document how to use the new UI verification capability. Update `docs/TESTING.md` with the high-level policy and add a dedicated document for the concrete runtime UI verification workflow if needed.

## Scope
- Keep `docs/TESTING.md` concise and authoritative at the policy level
- Add a dedicated workflow/reference doc if that is clearer than overloading `docs/TESTING.md`
- Cover both automated scenario use and fast manual UI review
- Document how to verify layout, navigation, and search interaction quickly

## Files to Inspect
- `docs/TESTING.md`
- new harness/scenario tooling from this epic
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `docs/TESTING.md` points clearly to the runtime UI verification workflow
- [ ] A dedicated doc exists if needed for concrete commands and scenarios
- [ ] Docs explain how to verify layout, navigation, search behavior, and external-tool flows
- [ ] A new operator/agent can run the basic workflow in a small number of commands after reading the docs
close_reason: Documented the runtime UI verification workflow with a dedicated runbook and clear links from testing policy docs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Task .5 documentation scope is reasonable but depends on knowing the final shape

**What**: Task .5 is straightforward — update TESTING.md and possibly add a dedicated doc. Its dependency on .3 and .4 is correct.

**Why**: No major concern here. The only risk is that if the epic scope changes significantly (e.g., collapsing tasks or deciding that in-process testing is sufficient), the documentation task needs to reflect the actual outcome, not the original plan.

**Suggested action**: This task is fine as-is. Minor suggestion: the acceptance criterion "docs are concise enough for regular agent/operator use" is subjective. Add a concrete length target like "the runtime verification section is <50 lines" or "a new operator can run a full verification in <3 commands after reading the docs."

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Status: implemented.
Outcome: kept docs/TESTING.md as policy/strategy and added a dedicated operator runbook at docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md with concrete automated/manual UI verification commands plus checklist coverage for layout, navigation, search, and external-tool flows.
Artifacts: docs/TESTING.md, docs/RUNTIME_UI_VERIFICATION.md.
Verification: go test ./internal/testing/ui ./internal/mode/search ./internal/app -run 'TestAssertionHelpersCoverStartupErrorsSearchAndActions|TestSearchModeReusableScenarioHelpersCoverTypingFragileAndClear|TestModelReusableBoardSearchDetailScenarioCoversTypingClearScrollAndBack|TestModelEmbeddedFixtureStartupLoadsBoardWithoutGatewaySectionErrors' -v\nNext step: acceptance review in gyg.6 can validate docs linkage, workflow coverage, speed, and stability.

title: Real app startup board queries fail under built binary
description:
## Summary
During verification of beads-workbench-dml, beads-workbench-6j6, and beads-workbench-n5o, a real PTY run of the built bwb binary against the seeded embedded fixture did not load board data cleanly.

## Reproduction
1. go build -o /tmp/bwb-verify ./cmd/bwb
2. Seed fixture repo with internal/testing/e2e/embeddedfixture/setup.sh
3. Run the built binary in a PTY from the fixture repo with BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1

## Observed
The UI renders board sections with error text such as:
- Error: blocked issues: command exited with code ...
- Error: ready issues: command exited with code 1: ...
- Error: list issues: command exited with code 1: ...
This prevents clean startup/full-app verification of user-facing workflows.

## Expected
The built app should load board data successfully in a real terminal session against the seeded fixture, matching the manual bd ready/bd blocked/bd list behavior and test expectations.

## Evidence
A PTY capture during verification showed board loading errors while the same fixture repo returned valid data for manual bd ready --json, bd blocked --json, and bd list --json commands.
close_reason: Fixed startup board query failures by serializing bd command runner to avoid embedded backend lock contention; added startup fixture regression coverage.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Implemented fix verification for beads-workbench-no6 and beads-workbench-flr.

Summary:
- no6: SearchIssues now uses bd list --json fallback when query text is empty and WorkState=any, avoiding illegal empty bd search invocation.
- flr: CommandRunner now serializes bd CLI execution (mutex) to prevent concurrent embedded backend lock collisions during startup board/search/detail loads.

Verification performed:
- Focused tests: internal/gateway/beads, internal/mode/search, internal/app (fixture-backed tests included).
- Broad tests: go test ./... (pass).
- Quality gates: go build ./cmd/bwb and go vet ./... (pass).
- Runtime checks:
  * Manual fixture CLI check confirms empty bd search fails with "search query is required" while list/ready/blocked work.
  * Built /tmp/bwb-verify run against seeded fixture confirms board loads real issues and no startup section error strings (no Error: blocked/ready/list, no exclusive lock).

Note: scripted terminal input did not reliably switch to search mode in captured alt-screen transcript, so runtime proof for empty-query search path is covered via fixture-backed integration test TestSearchModeEmbeddedFixtureInitUsesEmptyQueryFallback plus gateway fallback tests.

title: Add scrolling for long detail content
description:
## Description
Detail mode currently renders issue content as a static block with no viewport/scroll support. If description, notes, comments, or dependency context exceed the available screen height, the user cannot scroll to inspect the rest of the content.

## Evidence
- User explicitly asked what happens when detail content does not fit on one page
- `internal/mode/details/model.go` is a thin render wrapper with no viewport state
- current codebase has no detail scroll/page navigation implementation

## Instructions
1. Add scrolling support for dedicated detail mode (and compact preview only if appropriate).
2. Ensure long descriptions/comments/dependencies remain readable in a constrained terminal.
3. Add tests for scroll behavior and rendered viewport state.
4. Keep `esc` back-to-browse behavior intact.

## Files to Inspect
- `internal/mode/details/`
- `internal/ui/details/`
- `internal/app/model.go`
- related detail/app tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Long detail content can be scrolled in dedicated detail mode
- [ ] Users can reach content below the first screenful
- [ ] Navigation remains smooth and does not break `esc` back behavior
- [ ] Tests cover long-content scrolling behavior
close_reason: Fixed: dedicated detail mode now supports scrolling long content with viewport state and key handling; tests added.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented dedicated detail-mode scrolling with viewport offset state and key handling (j/k, arrows, pgup/pgdn, home/end), plus scroll clamping on resize/content changes and reset on selection change. Added detail/app tests for long-content scrolling and verified with focused tests and full go test ./....

title: Clarify and improve launcher round-trip UX for terminal tools
description:
## Description
Launcher behavior is currently fire-and-forget for non-editor tools. This leaves unclear runtime UX for interactive terminal tools: how the user gets back to the app, whether launcher commands should take over the terminal, and what the expected post-launch workflow is. The current implementation starts launcher subprocesses immediately and does not provide a structured round-trip back into the app.

## Evidence
- User explicitly asked how to go back to the app after launcher use and what happens after saving changes
- `internal/launcher/process_runner.go` starts subprocesses and returns immediately
- current launcher UX is under-specified for interactive terminal tools

## Instructions
1. Define the intended UX for launchers vs rich editor flow.
2. Decide whether built-in launchers are meant for non-interactive fire-and-forget commands, terminal takeover, or a managed return flow.
3. Implement the chosen behavior or, at minimum, make the UX explicit and coherent in-app/docs.
4. Add tests for the intended launcher lifecycle behavior where practical.

## Files to Inspect
- `internal/launcher/`
- `internal/app/model.go`
- `docs/CODING.md`
- related launcher tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Launcher lifecycle/return behavior is clearly defined
- [ ] The app/docs no longer leave ambiguity about returning to the app after launcher use
- [ ] Interactive launcher behavior is either supported properly or explicitly constrained/documented
- [ ] Tests and docs reflect the intended launcher UX
close_reason: Fixed: launcher/editor lifecycle is now explicit and coherent in docs/help/toasts; tests updated for expected UX messaging.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Clarified launcher UX contract and in-app messaging: launchers are explicit background fire-and-forget with no managed return flow, while e remains the round-trip edit/save path with detail reload. Updated docs/CODING.md, shell help/header text, success toast messaging, and added tests covering lifecycle guidance.

title: Acceptance blocker: yze.4 epic remains open
description:
While re-verifying beads-workbench-yze.1 after yze.4.6 passed, the product acceptance criterion "All child epics and their acceptance review tasks are closed" still fails because beads-workbench-yze.4 is OPEN even though all of its child tasks and yze.4.6 are closed.

This blocks closing yze.1 on current beads state despite the verified test/build/vet evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-workbench-yze.4 is explicitly closed, or yze.1 acceptance criteria are updated to reflect the intended closure requirement
- [ ] beads-workbench-yze.1 can be re-verified without this tracker-state blocker
close_reason: Resolved by closing yze.4 after acceptance passed

title: Editor and launcher docs are stale relative to implementation
description: Acceptance review found documentation drift. docs/CODING.md runtime configuration still says Launcher.Definitions defaults to one built-in editor action, but internal/config/model.go now defines editor, nvim, opencode, and shell-command launchers. The docs also do not document the implemented launcher interpolation surface in a user-facing way sufficient for the acceptance criterion that documentation reflect editor/launcher behavior, config, and testing strategy.
close_reason: Updated docs/CODING.md to reflect current launcher defaults, interpolation surface, shell editor/launcher behavior, and related testing references; validated with focused config/launcher/app tests and architecture guardrail test.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Updated docs/CODING.md to match implemented behavior: documented four built-in launcher defaults (editor/nvim/opencode/shell-command), interpolation placeholders/scope (command/args/env/workdir), shell hotkey behavior (e/n/p/l), and test references for config, launcher interpolation, app hotkey wiring, and editor service seam. Validation run: go test ./internal/config ./internal/launcher, go test ./internal/app -run '^(TestModelBuiltInLauncherHotkeysUseLauncherService|TestModelEditHotkeyUsesEditorService|TestModelEditHotkeyInDetailModeUsesEditorService|TestNewServicesBuildsLauncherFromConfigDefinitions)$', and go test ./cmd/bwb -run TestArchitectureGuardrails.

title: Embedded smoke coverage omits editor or launcher workflow
description: Acceptance review found no embedded-mode smoke/e2e test that exercises an editor or launcher workflow. The only embedded fixture app tests currently present are TestModelEmbeddedFixtureBoardToDetailSmokeWorkflow and TestModelEmbeddedFixtureFullBoardCaptureGolden in internal/app/model_test.go; both cover browse/detail rendering only. This leaves acceptance criterion 'At least one embedded-mode editor/launcher smoke/e2e workflow passes' unmet.
close_reason: Added deterministic embedded fixture smoke test for detail edit hotkey launcher flow; acceptance criterion now covered and focused embedded tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented embedded-mode launcher smoke coverage in internal/app/model_test.go via TestModelEmbeddedFixtureDetailEditHotkeyLaunchesEditorAction. Workflow seeds embedded fixture repo, boots app with real bd gateway, enters Detail mode from board selection, triggers edit hotkey, and asserts launcher action=editor for fixture issue bwf-2. Focused verification passed: go test ./internal/app -run 'TestModelEmbeddedFixture(BoardToDetailSmokeWorkflow|DetailEditHotkeyLaunchesEditorAction|FullBoardCaptureGolden)$' -v and go test ./internal/app -run 'TestModelEditIssueActionUsesLauncherServiceFromDetailSelection|TestModelLaunchEditorActionUsesLauncherService' -v. Also attempted broader go test ./internal/app twice; timed out in this environment (120s and 300s).

title: Clean up orphaned goldens and duplicated rendering helpers
description:
## Description
Two cross-cutting code quality issues found during UX review that should be cleaned up.

### 1. Orphaned golden files
internal/app/testdata/model_startup.golden and internal/app/testdata/model_loading_initial.golden reference an OLD sidebar-based layout. No test references them — they are dead files.

### 2. Code duplication between board and search renderers
The following functions are fully duplicated between internal/ui/board/board.go and internal/ui/search/search.go:
- compactIssueType()
- compactPriority()
- compactIssueState()
- compactIssueID()
- normalizeToken()
- maxInt()

## Instructions
1. Delete the two orphaned golden files
2. Extract the duplicated rendering helpers into a shared package (e.g., internal/ui/shared/ or internal/ui/styles/)
3. Update board and search renderers to use the shared helpers
4. Verify all tests still pass

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No orphaned golden files in internal/app/testdata/
- [ ] No duplicated rendering helper functions between board and search
- [ ] All existing tests pass after extraction
close_reason: Deleted orphaned app goldens and extracted duplicated board/search rendering helpers into shared internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers package; board/search now consume shared helpers and all tests pass.

title: go vet fails in issue edit document formatter
description: Repository verification found that go vet fails in internal/domain/issue_edit_document.go because a fmt.Sprintf call uses %d for a string value (issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related)). This currently breaks the documented quality gate.
acceptance_criteria:
- go vet ./... succeeds
- The issue edit document formatter uses correct format verbs for all arguments
close_reason: Verified formatter and go vet fix

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Bug is accurate and trivially verified — go vet confirms the exact failure

**What**: Running "go vet ./..." produces: "internal/domain/issue_edit_document.go:101:13: fmt.Sprintf format %d has arg issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related) of wrong type string". Line 101 uses %d for the Related field (a string from issueEditRefsInline), while line 133 uses len(issue.Comments) for the actual integer. The format string has %s on line 100 for Related and %d on line 101 for Comments — but the argument order at lines 132-133 passes Related (string) at position 101 and len(Comments) (int) at position after. The format verb on line 100 is correct (%s for Blocks), line 101 says %d but receives issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related) which is a string. The fix: line 100 should be %s (Related), line 101 should be %d (Comments count) — checking the arg order shows Related comes before Comments, so the format on line 101 should be %s for Related and the Comments count needs its own %d. Actually the issue is simpler: line 100 already has "%s" for Related but the ARGUMENT at that position is Blocks. Let me re-read... Lines 98-101: "- Blocked By: %s / - Blocks: %s / - Related: %s / - Comments: %d". Lines 130-133: issueEditRefsInline(issue.BlockedBy) / issueEditRefsInline(issue.Blocks) / issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related) / len(issue.Comments). So the format/argument order is correct — the fix is literally just confirming. Yes, go vet says %d at line 101 gets a string. But looking at the actual positional arg, line 100 "- Related: %s" matches issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related) and line 101 "- Comments: %d" matches len(issue.Comments). Hmm, go vet says %d gets issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related). This means the argument alignment is off — there's a format/argument positional mismatch somewhere earlier in the Sprintf.

**Why**: This is a quality gate blocker per CODING.md: "go vet ./..." is a minimum quality gate. It also means "go test ./..." fails for the domain package (vet failures cause test compilation errors). This is a 1-line fix.

**Suggested action**: The description and acceptance criteria are clear and sufficient. The fix should trace the exact positional mismatch in the Sprintf call — it may be that an earlier format specifier was added without a corresponding argument, shifting all subsequent positions. A careful count of %s/%d verbs vs arguments in the Sprintf starting at line 68 is needed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Root cause identified: missing format verb for issueEditMarkerEditableEnd — all read-only fields are shifted

**What**: The Sprintf template has 30 format verbs but 31 arguments. The issueEditMarkerEditableEnd (argument 23 at line 125) has no corresponding %s in the template. This shifts all subsequent arguments by one position: the ID field receives EditableEnd marker text, Created At receives the ID, and so on until Related (string) lands on the %d verb for Comments. This means the rendered document has wrong values for ALL read-only context fields, not just a format verb mismatch.

**Why**: The go vet error ("format %d has arg issueEditRefsInline(issue.Related)") is the symptom. The disease is that every read-only field in the rendered document displays the wrong value. If anyone is using the editor flow, they would see the editable-end marker where the ID should be, the ID where Created At should be, etc. The fix is to add "%s" (or equivalent) between the Labels section end and the Read-Only Context header — a line like "%s\n" for the editable end marker, between current lines 91 and 93.

**Suggested action**: No change to the bug description needed — the acceptance criteria ("go vet succeeds" and "correct format verbs") will catch the fix. But the implementer should verify the round-trip by checking that RenderIssueEditDocument produces correct read-only field values in the existing golden/unit tests, not just that vet passes.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier PASS: RenderIssueEditDocument now includes the missing format verb for the editable-end marker, read-only context values are no longer shifted, focused internal/domain tests pass, and go vet ./... succeeds.

title: Detail view is not reloaded after a successful editor update
description: Focused app verification found that a successful editor update does not trigger a gateway reload of the current issue detail. In the edit success path, ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd returns nil because detail.Loading is set before re-requesting the current detail.
acceptance_criteria:
- After a successful editor update, the shell re-requests the selected issue detail from the gateway
- TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail passes
close_reason: Verified detail reload after editor update

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Bug is confirmed by code inspection — the guard clause in ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd is the exact cause

**What**: In the editIssueResultMsg handler (model.go line 165), m.detail.Loading is set to true, then ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd() is called (line 169). Inside ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd (line 320), the guard "if m.detail.Loading && m.detail.TargetID == selection.Issue.ID { return nil }" fires because: (a) Loading was just set to true on line 165, and (b) TargetID was set to the same issue ID during the original detail load. Result: the function returns nil, no gateway reload happens, and the detail view shows stale pre-edit data.

**Why**: The description correctly identifies the mechanism. The acceptance criteria are adequate: "re-requests the selected issue detail from the gateway" and "TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail passes."

**Suggested action**: The fix is straightforward: either (a) clear m.detail.TargetID before calling ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd so the guard doesn't match, or (b) use loadDetailCmd directly instead of going through ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd (which is designed for selection-change dedup, not forced reload). Option (b) is cleaner because ensureDetailForCurrentSelectionCmd's purpose is to avoid redundant loads — a post-edit reload is intentionally NOT redundant. The description and criteria are sufficient for implementation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## This bug is not independently verifiable until 9t8 is fixed

**What**: The acceptance criterion says "TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail passes." This test (model_test.go line 328) creates services via NewServices which constructs a real ProcessOpener. Although this specific test injects a FakeEditor at line 344-345 (bypassing the real opener), the test file shares the package with TestModelLaunchEditorActionUsesLauncherService which hangs. Running "go test ./internal/app/..." will timeout before reaching this test.

**Why**: Bug ybx cannot be verified in CI or local test runs until bug 9t8 is fixed. The two bugs have an implicit dependency that is not tracked.

**Suggested action**: Add a dependency: ybx depends-on 9t8 (or at minimum note that 9t8 must be fixed first). Alternatively, the test can be run in isolation with "go test -run TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail ./internal/app/" — but this is fragile and not how the quality gates work.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reviewer/verifier confirmed this bug is real and precise, but it is effectively blocked for package-level verification until beads-workbench-9t8 is fixed because the launcher miswiring currently breaks/hangs internal/app tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier PASS: after a successful editor update the shell now re-requests selected issue detail from the gateway, and TestModelEditIssueActionUsesEditorServiceAndUpdatesDetail passes.
