title: CreateIssue cannot express bd priority 0
description: domain.CreateIssueInput stores Priority as an int and writes.go only appends --priority when input.Priority != 0. Because bd create accepts 0 as the highest priority, the gateway cannot create P0 issues: passing 0 is treated as omitted and bd falls back to its default priority instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- CreateIssue can explicitly request bd priority 0
- Omitted priority remains distinguishable from explicit priority 0
- Tests cover create with explicit priority 0 against command argument construction
close_reason: Made create priority explicit with pointer semantics and P0 test coverage

title: Gateway search/query models are missing Phase 1 structured filters
description: The current domain SearchIssuesQuery and related gateway shapes expose text, status, type, assignee, and labels, but do not expose priority, ready, or blocked filters that the product docs call out for the standalone search/narrowing experience. This leaves a mismatch between the gateway contract and the downstream Phase 1/Phase 3 UI expectations.
acceptance_criteria:
- Gateway query models support priority filtering needed by search/browse flows
- Gateway query models support ready and blocked narrowing without UI-layer shelling out
- The chosen API shape is documented and covered by tests
close_reason: Extended gateway query models for priority and ready/blocked filtering with documented CLI-backed behavior

title: No concrete CLI gateway implements BeadsGateway
description: Read methods are implemented on internal/gateway/beads.CLIGateway while write methods are implemented on internal/gateway/beads.Gateway. The shared interface in internal/gateway/beads/interface.go expects one BeadsGateway with both read and write methods, so Phase 1 consumers cannot construct a single CLI-backed implementation that satisfies the interface.
acceptance_criteria:
- One concrete CLI-backed type implements all methods on BeadsGateway
- Construction API for the CLI gateway returns a BeadsGateway-compatible implementation
- Read and write tests run against the same concrete gateway type
close_reason: Unified read and write methods onto one CLI gateway implementation

title: IssueSummary assignee mapping does not match bd list/ready/search JSON
description: The gateway maps IssueSummary.Assignee from the JSON field 'assignee' in issueSummaryFromMap(), but actual bd list --json, bd ready --json, and bd search --json output in this repo exposes the field as 'owner'. As a result, assignee data is dropped from read/search summaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- ListIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- ReadyIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- SearchIssues maps assignee/owner data from the official bd JSON surface into IssueSummary.Assignee
- Tests cover the real field shape returned by bd
close_reason: Mapped IssueSummary assignee from official owner field with fallback coverage

title: Found: missing Go module deps break vet/test in ui overlay
description: Discovered while working on beads-workbench-omd.3. Running go vet ./... and go test ./... fails in internal/ui/overlay and internal/ui/styles because go.mod does not include github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss and github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi.
close_reason: Resolved by adding required UI module dependencies during yze.2.2

title: capture_bwb_screen times out on open status modal
description: Discovered while verifying beads-workbench-axe. scripts/capture_bwb_screen.py can time out when the app opens a text-input modal and remains on that screen. Repro: build /tmp/bwb, seed embedded fixture, then run the capture script with steps 3, RIGHT, ENTER to open the status modal. Actual: the script times out after 10s even though the modal is visibly open. Expected: the capture path should complete successfully for modal states or provide a deterministic completion condition that does not depend on the screen becoming idle.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reproducing the status-modal capture path no longer times out solely because the modal stays open
- The capture script still terminates the child process cleanly on success and timeout
- The expected modal screen is capturable in a deterministic verification run

title: Enrich related issue references for left rail
description:
## Description
Expose the metadata needed for left-rail rows by enriching related issue references from `bd show --json`.

## Instructions
1. Extend `domain.IssueReference` to carry issue type, priority, and status in addition to id and title.
2. Update gateway parsing in `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` so references parsed from `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `related` preserve `issue_type`, `priority`, and `status` when present.
3. Keep compatibility with existing detail rendering callers that only read id and title.
4. Add or update tests covering the richer reference mapping from `bd show --json`.
5. Do not distinguish Children in this task; parent-child relationship separation remains out of scope for v1.

## Files to Modify
- internal/domain/issues.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `IssueReference` includes type, priority, and status fields needed by the rail row renderer
- [ ] `bd show` dependency and related parsing preserves those fields when present
- [ ] Existing detail rendering behavior continues to work with richer references
- [ ] Children remain out of scope for this task
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/gateway/beads
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: Add detail-mode rail navigation and related issue open behavior
description:
## Description
Add dedicated detail-mode keyboard navigation for the left rail and allow opening related issues directly from it.

## Instructions
1. Update the shell-owned detail presentation state and app integration so dedicated detail mode tracks pane focus across the left rail, content pane, and metadata rail.
2. Reuse existing detail scroll bindings as focus-conditional behavior: when the content pane is focused, up and down keep scrolling exactly as today; when the left rail is focused, up and down move the selected related issue row. Metadata remains non-interactive in v1.
3. Use left and right to move pane focus between left rail, content, and metadata.
4. When Enter is pressed on a focused left-rail item, emit a mode-level open-related-issue intent from `internal/mode/details/model.go`. Handle that intent in `internal/app/model.go` by loading the selected related issue and keeping dedicated detail mode active.
5. Preserve current content scrolling behavior and scroll restoration when moving between issues.
6. Keep search preview and compact detail rendering non-interactive.
7. Add or update tests for focus movement, related-issue open behavior, and existing detail-mode round-trip expectations.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mode/details/model.go
- internal/mode/details/model_test.go
- internal/app/model.go
- internal/app/model_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Left, right, up, down, and Enter support the approved detail-mode rail interaction model
- [ ] Up and down remain content scroll controls when the content pane is focused and become row-selection controls when the left rail is focused
- [ ] Enter on a focused left-rail item loads the selected related issue through a mode-level intent and keeps dedicated detail mode active
- [ ] Content-pane scrolling and scroll restoration continue to work as before
- [ ] Search preview and compact detail remain non-interactive summaries
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/mode/details ./internal/app
close_reason: Implemented detail-mode pane focus navigation, left-rail related issue open intent handling, and coverage for focus/open round-trips.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Keybinding conflict not addressed. What: detail context currently binds j/down to scroll_down and k/up to scroll_up (config/keybindings.go:144-145). Task says up/down should select rail rows when rail is focused, but up/down already mean scroll. The task says 'preserve current content scrolling behavior when the content pane is focused' but does not specify how the same keys dispatch differently per focused pane. Why: a tasker must design the focus-aware key dispatch from scratch since the current HandleKey in mode/details/model.go has no concept of pane focus. This is a design decision disguised as an implementation task. Suggested action: add explicit instructions for how pane focus changes key dispatch — either (a) new detail actions like rail_move_up/rail_move_down that share the same default bindings but dispatch based on focus state, or (b) reuse existing scroll_up/scroll_down with focus-conditional behavior documented in the task.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Enter key conflict with shell-level issue actions. What: task says Enter opens a selected related issue from the rail. But the app model (internal/app/model.go) handles key dispatch at the shell level before mode-specific handling. If Enter is already consumed by the shell for other actions in detail mode, the rail's Enter will never fire. Why: the task's files-to-modify list includes app/model.go which suggests the author is aware of shell-level dispatch, but the instructions don't describe how Enter dispatch precedence works when the rail is focused vs when it is not. Suggested action: add an instruction that rail Enter is dispatched as a mode-level intent (a tea.Cmd or custom message) from mode/details back to the app, not as a direct key match in app/model.go.

title: Add narrow related issue row renderer for left rail
description:
## Description
Add a shared one-line related-issue row renderer that stays readable inside a 22-28 column left rail.

## Instructions
1. Build on the enriched `domain.IssueReference` metadata from the prerequisite reference-mapping task; do not change gateway or domain mapping in this task unless a tiny adapter is required at the renderer boundary.
2. Update shared compact issue row rendering so the detail left rail can show type, priority, compact status, compact issue id, and a truncated title on one dense line.
3. Keep the renderer list-oriented and compact; do not introduce tree glyphs, nesting, or multi-line row layouts.
4. Ensure selected and unselected rows remain visually distinct for detail-mode focus.
5. Add or update tests covering narrow widths in the 22-28 column range.

## Files to Modify
- internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go
- internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go
- internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers.go
- internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Related-issue rows remain one line wide and readable at 22, 24, and 28 columns
- [ ] Rendered rows include type, priority, status, compact issue id, and truncated title
- [ ] Selected rows remain visibly distinct from unselected rows
- [ ] No tree glyphs, nesting markers, or multi-line layouts are introduced
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/ui/shared/...
close_reason: Implemented shared compact related-issue row renderer for narrow 22-28 column rails with metadata tokens, selection contrast, and focused tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Data model gap blocks row rendering. What: acceptance criteria say rows must include type, priority, status, compact issue id, and truncated title. But domain.IssueReference only has ID and Title. The gateway's referencesFromMapArray discards status, priority, and issue_type from bd show --json output even though bd already provides them. The shared RenderCompact in issuerow takes domain.IssueSummary not IssueReference. Why: a tasker picking up syf.3 will discover they cannot render the specified fields because the data does not exist in the domain model. This is a hidden prerequisite. Suggested action: either (a) add a prerequisite task to enrich IssueReference with Status/Type/Priority and update the gateway parser, or (b) explicitly reduce the row spec to ID + truncated title only and adjust acceptance criteria.

title: Implement wide-screen detail layout with left related issues rail
description:
## Description
Implement the wide-screen detail renderer for the approved left related-issues rail.

## Instructions
1. Add a dedicated 3-column detail layout that activates only on wide terminals at about 140 columns or wider.
2. Keep the left rail compact at roughly 20 percent of available width, clamped to 22-28 columns, and keep the existing metadata rail at 34 columns.
3. Render the left rail as always-expanded grouped lists in this order: Blocked by, Blocks, Related. Render only non-empty groups.
4. Reuse the shared rail row renderer rather than duplicating row formatting logic inside the detail view.
5. Focus-pane styling in the renderer must be input-driven only. Default visual focus to the content pane until the navigation task wires active pane movement.
6. When the left rail is active in wide layout, suppress the duplicate inline Related Work section from the main content column. Continue showing inline related-work sections in fallback layouts below the wide breakpoint.
7. Preserve current two-column and single-column behavior below the wide-screen breakpoint.
8. Keep v1 scope explicit: no tree presentation, no nesting, no children group.
9. Add or update golden tests for wide-screen left-rail rendering and below-breakpoint fallback behavior.

## Files to Modify
- internal/ui/details/details.go
- internal/ui/details/details_test.go
- internal/ui/details/metadata.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detail mode switches to a 3-column layout at the wide-screen breakpoint and keeps existing fallback layouts below it
- [ ] Left rail width stays within the approved compact range and the content column remains dominant
- [ ] Left rail renders grouped Blocked by, Blocks, and Related lists using the shared row renderer
- [ ] Wide layout avoids duplicating the same related-work lists in the content body
- [ ] Renderer remains tree-free: no nesting, connectors, or collapsible groups
- [ ] Golden coverage captures the left-rail layout and fallback behavior
- [ ] Tests pass: go test ./internal/ui/details
close_reason: Implemented wide 3-column detail layout with compact related-issues left rail, preserved fallback layouts, and added/updated goldens and tests.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Left/right focus bindings not in scope but needed. What: this task adds the 3-column layout and says 'extend the detail renderer input state only as needed to show focused-pane and selected-row styling.' But left/right arrow keys are not currently bound in the detail context (they are only in board and modal). The task does not mention adding new detail keybinding actions. syf.5 handles keyboard navigation but depends on syf.4, meaning syf.4 will ship a layout with focus styling but no way to actually change focus. Why: the acceptance criteria for syf.4 say 'Golden coverage captures the left-rail layout' but those goldens will need to show focus states that cannot be triggered by keyboard in syf.4 alone. This is fine if syf.4 is understood as purely rendering, but the 'focused-pane styling' instruction creates ambiguity. Suggested action: clarify in syf.4 that focused-pane styling is driven by input state props (not by keyboard handling) and that focus is hardcoded to content pane until syf.5 wires navigation.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Related Work duplication. What: the current detail renderer already shows related work inline in the content sections (renderContentSections calls renderReferences for Blocked by, Blocks, Related at lines 156-158 of details.go). When the wide-screen left rail is active, the same references will appear twice — once in the left rail and once inline in the content body. Why: users will see duplicate information, wasting screen real estate and creating confusion. Suggested action: add an instruction to suppress or collapse the inline Related Work section in renderContentSections when the wide-screen left rail is active.

title: Research left related issues rail UX and interaction model
description:
## Description
Research complete for a compact wide-screen left related-issues rail distinct from the donor tree view. This task defines the recommended layout and interaction model so implementation tasks can be created without ambiguity.

## Recommendation
### Layout
- Enable a 3-column detail layout only on wide terminals at about 140 columns or wider.
- Allocate the left rail at roughly 20 percent of available width, clamped to 22-28 columns.
- Keep the existing right metadata rail at 34 columns.
- Keep the main content column as the dominant pane. If width falls below the breakpoint, fall back to the current 2-column or single-column detail layout and keep related work inline as today.

### Related groups
- v1 groups: Blocked by, Blocks, Related.
- Do not show a tree, nesting, connectors, or expand/collapse affordances.
- Do not show Children in v1 until gateway/domain parsing can distinguish parent-child dependents from ordinary blocking dependents.
- When the data model gap is addressed later, insert Children as its own group between Blocks and Related.

### Interaction model
- Dedicated detail mode only: the left rail should be keyboard navigable.
- Left/right changes focus across left rail, content, and metadata panes.
- Up/down moves within the active rail list; Enter opens the selected issue in detail mode.
- Search preview and compact detail views remain read-only summaries rather than interactive rail surfaces.
- Mouse support can remain limited to scroll for v1; no donor-style tree interactions.

### Row content
- Use a single-line compact row derived from shared issuerow/renderhelpers.
- Show type, priority, compact status, compact issue id, and a truncated title.
- Prefer one dense line per issue over two-line rows or metadata-heavy badges.
- Keep groups always expanded in v1 to avoid extra state and visual noise.

### Donor boundaries
- Reuse only the donor ideas of grouped dependency lists and pane focus.
- Do not port the donor tree widget, dependency tree mode toggle, or children/deps tree presentation.

## Current data model constraints
- `internal/domain.IssueDetail` currently exposes `BlockedBy`, `Blocks`, and `Related` only.
- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` maps all `bd show` dependents into `Blocks` and does not preserve `dependency_type`.
- `bd show --json` already contains `dependency_type=parent-child` for child issues, so a future task can parse and expose `Children` separately.

## Follow-up implementation tasks after approval
1. Add relationship-aware detail data mapping and tests if Children will be shown.
2. Implement a 3-column detail renderer with wide-screen left-rail fallback behavior.
3. Add detail-mode pane focus and navigation behavior for rail/content/metadata.
4. Extend shared compact issue row rendering for the narrower left-rail width and add golden coverage.

## Files to Inspect or Likely Modify Later
- internal/ui/details/details.go
- internal/ui/details/metadata.go
- internal/mode/details/model.go
- internal/domain/issues.go
- internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go
- internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Recommendation covers layout, width, groups, and navigation model
- [x] Recommendation explicitly avoids donor style full tree rendering
- [x] Recommendation documents any current data model constraints and follow up work
- [x] Follow up implementation tasks can be created without further ambiguity
close_reason: Research recommendation documented

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Domain model gap: children and parent-child relationships not parsed by gateway. What: syf.1 open questions include 'which groups must appear in the rail: blocked by, blocks, children, related'. However, the current domain model (IssueDetail) has BlockedBy, Blocks, and Related — but no Children or Parent fields. The bd show JSON does expose children via the dependents array with dependency_type='parent-child', but the gateway flattens all dependents into Blocks without distinguishing parent-child from blocks relationships. Why: the research task needs to know this constraint exists. Any left-rail design that shows children as a separate group will require gateway and domain model changes first. Suggested action: add this data model limitation as a known constraint in the research task so the recommendation accounts for it.

title: Acceptance Review: Left related issues navigation rail
description:
## Description
Verify the implemented left related-issues rail before the epic is closed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dedicated detail mode renders the grouped left rail only at the intended wide-screen breakpoint and falls back cleanly below it
- [ ] Left rail uses dense one-line rows for Blocked by, Blocks, and Related that show type, priority, status, compact id, and truncated title without donor-style tree rendering
- [ ] Wide layout does not duplicate the same related-work lists inline in the content body
- [ ] Keyboard navigation can focus the rail and open a selected related issue while preserving existing detail scrolling behavior
- [ ] Search preview and compact detail presentations remain non-interactive summaries
- [ ] Relevant tests and quality gates pass for the shipped implementation

## Owner
verifier
close_reason: All criteria verified; tests, quality gates, and runtime checks passed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier rerun: BLOCKED. Focused tests + repo quality gates pass, but real runtime verification against /tmp/tmp.68EmxXufy0 shows a bd --type related link is dropped from IssueDetail.Related, so the wide left rail does not render the Related group from actual CLI output. Tracked in beads-workbench-syf.7. Next: fix gateway show parsing for real related-link JSON shape, then rerun acceptance.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Verifier rerun: PASS. Wide-rail breakpoint/fallback, dense grouped rows, no wide inline duplication, rail navigation/open, and non-interactive search preview verified. Focused tests + go build/vet/test passed. Artifact: /tmp/beads-workbench-syf2-runtime-wva40ex6

title: Left related issues navigation rail
description:
## Description
Rework issue inspection UX around a true side panel model instead of treating related issue navigation as a sub-surface inside the issue detail view.

## Updated Product Direction
- Related issue browsing should behave like its own side panel, analogous to browsing issues in the dashboard while a detail pane updates beside it.
- Selecting issues in that side panel should update the main issue detail without forcing a separate nested focus model inside the detail body.
- Focus/navigation behavior should become consistent with the rest of the app rather than introducing a detail-only interaction pattern.
- Detail metadata fields may become directly actionable in the future, with Enter-driven edits for fields like status and priority.
- Layout should use the available screen width more effectively; the current implementation under-uses horizontal space and feels boxed in.

## Current State
The shipped v1 left rail works functionally and is verified, but it does not match the preferred UX direction above. Treat the current implementation as an intermediate step, not the final interaction model.

## Next UX Questions to Resolve
- How should the inspector/side-panel layout be partitioned across board, search, and dedicated detail so focus behavior is uniform?
- Should the issue list panel become a persistent browser context while the detail pane updates live on selection?
- Which metadata fields should support direct Enter-to-edit actions in v1 of inline editing?
- How should the layout expand to better use full terminal width without making content harder to read?
close_reason: all steps complete

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Epic scope gap: no implementation tasks after research. What: the epic has only two children — syf.1 (research) and syf.2 (acceptance review). There are zero implementation tasks. Why: after the research task resolves the design questions, someone still needs to create implementation tasks. The epic's success criteria say 'follow up implementation tasks can be created without ambiguity' but this is a success criterion for the research task, not the epic. The epic's own success criteria don't describe a working left rail, only a research output. Suggested action: clarify whether this epic is intentionally research-only (in which case rename it to reflect that) or whether implementation tasks will be added after syf.1 completes (in which case add an explicit note that the epic will be expanded after research approval).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Research complete in beads-workbench-syf.1. Outcome: recommend a 3-column detail layout at about >=140 cols with a 22-28 col left rail; v1 groups are Blocked by / Blocks / Related; children stay out of v1 until gateway/domain preserve parent-child dependency_type. Next: create implementation tasks after design approval.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implementation planning created from approved research. Added row-renderer, layout, and navigation tasks for v1 left rail. Children remain out of scope for v1 until gateway/domain preserve parent-child relationship types. Reviewer requested on new task set.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing prerequisite: IssueReference enrichment. What: the implementation plan assumes rail rows will show type, priority, and status (from research syf.1 row content spec), but domain.IssueReference only carries ID and Title. The gateway already receives these fields from bd show --json but discards them. None of the three implementation tasks include gateway or domain model changes in their files-to-modify lists. Why: this is a blocking gap — syf.3 cannot render the specified row format without data that doesn't exist in the model. Suggested action: add a prerequisite task (before syf.3) to enrich IssueReference with Type/Status/Priority fields and update the gateway parser, or descope the row renderer to ID+title only.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Reviewer findings incorporated: added prerequisite task beads-workbench-syf.6 for IssueReference metadata enrichment and clarified syf.3, syf.4, and syf.5 to resolve row-data, duplication, focus, and Enter-dispatch ambiguities before execution.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Post-implementation UX feedback changed direction: the left issue browser should become a true side panel rather than a nested focusable area inside issue detail. Desired model is closer to dashboard browsing where selection updates adjacent detail. Also noted: focus behavior should be consistent app-wide, metadata fields could become Enter-editable (status/priority are good candidates), and the current layout under-uses full screen width.

title: Deduplicate gateway read methods: extract buildFilterArgs helper
description:
## Description
`internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` has ~60 lines of near-identical filter flag assembly code repeated 3 times across `SearchIssues`, `searchIssuesFromList`, and `ListIssues`. There's also a duplicate `[]IssueSummary → []SearchResult` conversion loop.

## Instructions

### 1. Extract buildFilterArgs helper
1. Open `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go`
2. Identify the repeated filter flag assembly pattern across the 3 methods
3. Extract a `buildFilterArgs(filter domain.IssueFilter) []string` function
4. Replace the 3 inline copies with calls to this helper

### 2. Extract toSearchResults conversion helper
1. Find the duplicate `[]IssueSummary → []SearchResult` conversion loops
2. Extract a `toSearchResults(issues []domain.IssueSummary) []domain.SearchResult` function
3. Replace the inline copies

### 3. Remove duplicate constructor
- `NewCLIGateway` and `NewGateway` — one is a trivial wrapper of the other
- Keep `NewGateway` (shorter, clearer), remove `NewCLIGateway` or make it an alias
- Update any callers of the removed constructor

### 4. Verify tests
- All existing gateway tests should pass unchanged (behavior not modified)

## Files to Modify
- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` (extract helpers, remove duplicate constructor)
- Any files that call `NewCLIGateway` (update to `NewGateway`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Filter arg assembly appears once, not three times
- [ ] SearchResult conversion appears once, not twice
- [ ] Only one constructor for the gateway
- [ ] `go test ./internal/gateway/beads/...` passes
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
close_reason: Completed deduplication in read_gateway.go, removed NewGateway in favor of NewCLIGateway, updated callers, and verified gateway tests + bwb build pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Duplicate scope with 0jc: both this task and 0jc instruction #4 claim the buildFilterArgs extraction from read_gateway.go. Why: two tasks with the same deliverable creates confusion and wasted effort. Suggested action: confirm this task is the sole owner of all read_gateway.go deduplication (buildFilterArgs, toSearchResults, constructor consolidation) and remove the overlapping item from 0jc.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Instruction #3 says 'NewCLIGateway and NewGateway — one is a trivial wrapper of the other. Keep NewGateway (shorter, clearer), remove NewCLIGateway.' But the actual code shows the OPPOSITE dependency: NewGateway calls NewCLIGateway (line 40), and NewCLIGateway has the nil-guard logic (line 31-33). All external callers use NewGateway (from model_test.go, search/model_test.go etc.) while internal callers use NewCLIGateway. Why: if the tasker follows instructions literally and removes NewCLIGateway, they lose the nil-guard. If they keep NewGateway, they need to move the nil-guard into it. Suggested action: specify that the nil-guard logic must be preserved in whichever constructor survives. Also note that the nil-guard itself is flagged for removal in 2t4 item #14 (writes.go nil-guard) — verify these are different nil-guards before removing either.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Execution paused: ticket appears stale/ambiguous. (1) Scope overlaps with task 0jc for buildFilterArgs extraction; ownership needs confirmation to avoid duplicate work. (2) Constructor instruction conflicts with current code: NewGateway delegates to NewCLIGateway, which contains nil-guard logic. Please clarify which constructor should own/preserve nil-guard behavior before implementation.

title: Go conventions cleanup: naming, exports, type safety
description:
## Description
Tighten Go conventions identified during code review. None of these are bugs, but they improve code quality, reduce API surface, and prevent future mistakes.

## Instructions

### 1. Fix stuttered name: dashboard.DashboardDefinitionProvider → dashboard.Provider
- `internal/dashboard/definition.go` — Rename `DashboardDefinitionProvider` interface to `Provider`
- Update all references in `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider.go` and any consumers (app, mode)
- Go convention: avoid repeating the package name in exported identifiers

### 2. Unexport types never used outside their package
- `internal/launcher/service.go` — Unexport `TemplateInterpolator` → `templateInterpolator` and `DefinitionResolver` → `definitionResolver` (never imported outside launcher package)

### 3. Replace reflect.DeepEqual with ID comparison
- `internal/app/model.go` (~line 523) — `selectionEqual` uses `reflect.DeepEqual(a.Issue, b.Issue)` — replace with `a.Issue.ID == b.Issue.ID`
- Remove `"reflect"` from imports if no longer needed

### 4. Add empty-issueID validation to write methods
- `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go` — `UpdateIssue`, `CloseIssue`, `AddComment` don't validate empty issue ID (inconsistent with `ShowIssue` which does)
- Add the same ID validation that ShowIssue uses

## Files to Modify
- `internal/dashboard/definition.go` (rename interface)
- `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider.go` (update type references)
- `internal/launcher/service.go` (unexport types)
- `internal/app/model.go` (replace reflect.DeepEqual, remove reflect import)
- `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go` (add ID validation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No stuttered names (dashboard.Provider, not dashboard.DashboardDefinitionProvider)
- [ ] No unnecessarily exported types in launcher
- [ ] reflect package removed from app/model.go imports
- [ ] Write methods validate empty issue IDs
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` succeeds
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
close_reason: Implemented Go conventions cleanup: provider rename, launcher unexports, ID-based selection compare, and issue ID validation in write methods; build and tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Instruction #1 (rename DashboardDefinitionProvider → Provider) will touch 3 files: definition.go, builtin_provider.go, and mode/board/model.go. This is a straightforward rename, BUT if 2t4 also modifies dashboard/builtin_provider.go (deleting actorResolver), there's a merge conflict risk. Both are Wave 2 (dsd depends on 2t4), so ordering is correct — but if the dead code removal changes line numbers or nearby context, the rename could conflict. Why: not a blocker, just noting that the tasker should work from fresh code after 2t4 completes. Suggested action: no change needed, the dependency is already correct.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Instruction #5 (consolidate catalog option types) and #6 (typed keybinding context) are marked 'optional' and 'discuss first'. These weaken the task's acceptance criteria — a tasker could skip both and still pass. Why: optional items in a chore task create ambiguity about 'done.' If they're not worth doing, remove them from the task. If they are, make them non-optional. Suggested action: either commit to these items or remove them. If keeping them optional, add explicit criteria like 'items #5 and #6 are optional; if skipped, add a comment explaining why.'

title: Improve alibi tests: toaster, loading, and add renderhelpers tests
description:
## Description
Several test files have alibi coverage — they prove the function exists but don't test meaningful behavior. Additionally, `internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers.go` has no test file at all, and `internal/domain/issue_edit_document_test.go` is missing edge case coverage.

## Instructions

### 1. Improve toaster tests (`internal/ui/toaster/toaster_test.go`)
Currently 34 lines that don't test:
- Different toast styles (error vs warning vs info vs success) produce visually distinct output
- Toast placement in the overlay
- Toast expiry/dismissal behavior
- Multiple concurrent toasts

Add tests for at least: distinct style output and toast content rendering.

### 2. Improve loading tests (`internal/ui/loading/loading_test.go`)
Currently 27 lines that miss:
- Search scope rendering (search-specific loading state)
- Summary/detail text edge cases
- Empty/nil state handling

Add tests for scope variants and edge cases.

### 3. Create renderhelpers tests (`internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers_test.go`)
No test file exists. Add tests for:
- `NormalizeToken` (the canonical token normalizer)
- `TruncateWithEllipsis` or similar truncation helpers
- Any other exported functions in the file

### 4. Add editor opener fallback chain test (`internal/launcher/editor/opener_test.go`)
Currently only tests `splitEditorCommand`. The documented fallback chain (EditorCommand → $EDITOR → vi) has zero coverage. Add tests that verify:
- Explicit EditorCommand is used when set
- $EDITOR env var is used when EditorCommand is empty
- `vi` fallback when both are empty

### 5. Add issue edit document edge case tests (`internal/domain/issue_edit_document_test.go`)
Original review found missing edge cases:
- ClearLabels path (all labels removed)
- Priority boundary values (0, 4, and out-of-range values)
- "P2" prefix parsing (user writes "P2" instead of "2")
- Assignee clearing (set to empty)
- Multi-paragraph descriptions
- Partial marker corruption (missing BWB:FIELD markers)

## Files to Modify
- `internal/ui/toaster/toaster_test.go` (improve)
- `internal/ui/loading/loading_test.go` (improve)
- `internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers_test.go` (create)
- `internal/launcher/editor/opener_test.go` (add fallback chain tests)
- `internal/domain/issue_edit_document_test.go` (add edge case tests)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toaster tests verify distinct style output
- [ ] Loading tests cover scope variants
- [ ] renderhelpers has test file with meaningful coverage
- [ ] Editor opener fallback chain tested (3 scenarios)
- [ ] Issue edit document edge cases tested (6 scenarios listed above)
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
close_reason: Added meaningful coverage for toaster/loading/renderhelpers/editor opener fallback and issue edit edge cases; created follow-up bug beads-workbench-asd for unrelated overlay test failures seen in go test ./...

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing coverage item: the original review identified 'issue edit edge cases: missing (ClearLabels, priority boundaries, assignee clearing)' as a test gap. This is not covered by 9uk or any other task in the epic. Why: these are write-path edge cases in the gateway that could silently corrupt issue data. Clearing labels vs setting empty labels, priority boundary validation, and assignee clearing are distinct behaviors that deserve test coverage. Suggested action: either add a sub-item to this task for issue edit edge case tests, or create a separate task. At minimum, acknowledge the deferral.

title: Extract shared test helpers and eliminate test duplication
description:
## Description
Several test helpers are copy-pasted across 2-3 test files. This creates drift risk and makes the test suite harder to maintain. The project already has `internal/testing/` for shared infrastructure.

## Instructions

### 1. Extract hasGatewayCall into testing/fakes
- Currently duplicated in:
  - `internal/app/model_test.go` (~line 1342)
  - `internal/mode/search/model_test.go` (~line 408)
  - `internal/launcher/editor/service_test.go` (~line 110, as `hasGatewayMethod`)
- Move to `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` as a method on FakeBeadsGateway: `HasCall(method string) bool`
- Update all 3 test files to use the shared version

### 2. Extract controllerAdapter into testing/ui
- Currently duplicated in:
  - `internal/mode/board/model_test.go`
  - `internal/mode/search/model_test.go`
- Move to `internal/testing/ui/` as a shared helper
- Update both test files

### 3. Extract ansiEscapePattern regex into a shared test constant
- Currently duplicated in:
  - `internal/ui/board/board_test.go` (~line 16)
  - `internal/ui/search/search_test.go` (~line 18)
  - `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go` (~line 13)
- Create a shared constant in `internal/testing/ui/assertions.go` or a new `internal/testing/ui/helpers.go`
- Update all 3 test files

### 4. Fix FakeProcessRunner: add compile-time interface assertion + delete local duplicate
- `internal/testing/fakes/process_runner.go` is missing `var _ launcher.ProcessRunner = (*FakeProcessRunner)(nil)`
- `internal/launcher/service_test.go` has a local `recordingRunner` that duplicates FakeProcessRunner — delete it and use the shared fake
- Add the compile-time assertion

## Files to Modify
- `internal/testing/fakes/beads_gateway.go` (add HasCall method)
- `internal/testing/fakes/process_runner.go` (add interface assertion)
- `internal/testing/ui/` (add shared helpers)
- `internal/app/model_test.go` (use shared hasGatewayCall)
- `internal/mode/search/model_test.go` (use shared helpers)
- `internal/mode/board/model_test.go` (use shared controllerAdapter)
- `internal/launcher/service_test.go` (use FakeProcessRunner, delete local duplicate)
- `internal/launcher/editor/service_test.go` (use shared hasGatewayCall)
- `internal/ui/board/board_test.go` (use shared regex)
- `internal/ui/search/search_test.go` (use shared regex)
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row_test.go` (use shared regex)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero duplicated test helpers across test files
- [ ] FakeProcessRunner has compile-time interface assertion
- [ ] Local recordingRunner in service_test.go deleted
- [ ] All shared helpers live in `internal/testing/`
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
close_reason: Extracted shared test helpers into internal/testing, removed duplicate helpers, and switched launcher tests to shared FakeProcessRunner with interface assertion.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Task scope is large: 11 files modified, 4 distinct extraction/consolidation operations, touching test infrastructure used by 8+ test files. This is the most cross-cutting non-bug task. Why: if any extraction breaks a test import or changes a helper signature, it cascades across all consuming test files. A partial failure is hard to recover from. Suggested action: not blocking, but the tasker should commit after each extraction group (1. hasGatewayCall, 2. controllerAdapter, 3. ansiEscapePattern, 4. FakeProcessRunner) and run go test ./... between each. Make this explicit in the instructions.

title: Eliminate DRY violations: duplicate helpers and functions
description:
## Description
The codebase has 6+ helper functions written 2-3 times each. This creates maintenance risk (change one, forget the other) and adds unnecessary code volume.

## Instructions

### 1. Replace maxInt/minInt with Go 1.24 builtins
Go 1.24 (which this project uses per go.mod) has `min()` and `max()` builtins. Replace all hand-rolled versions:
- `internal/ui/modal/modal.go` (~line 412) — delete `maxInt`, replace calls with `max()`
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go` (~line 80) — delete `maxInt`/`minInt`, replace calls with `max()`/`min()`
- `internal/mode/details/model.go` — has `maxInt`, replace calls with `max()`
- `internal/app/model.go` — has `maxInt`, replace calls with `max()`

### 2. Replace containsString/contains with slices.Contains
- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` (~line 433) — delete `containsString`, use `slices.Contains`
- `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` — delete hand-rolled `contains`, use `slices.Contains`
- Add `"slices"` to imports where needed

### 3. Consolidate normalizeIssueToken / NormalizeToken
- `internal/ui/styles/colors.go` (~line 159) has `normalizeIssueToken`
- `internal/ui/shared/renderhelpers/helpers.go` (~line 121) has `NormalizeToken`
- These are identical. Keep one (in renderhelpers since it's the shared package), delete the other, and import it
- If `normalizeIssueToken` in colors.go is unexported and used locally, make it call the shared version or move the logic

### 4. Deduplicate MergeKeyBindings nil-check pattern
- `internal/config/keybindings.go` `MergeKeyBindings` repeats the same 3-line nil-check/range/clone pattern 5 times (once per context)
- Extract a helper: `mergeContextBindings(base, override map[string][]string) map[string][]string`

**NOTE:** Gateway filter arg-building dedup is owned by beads-workbench-ia6. Do NOT touch `read_gateway.go` filter assembly here — only the `containsString` helper.

After each group of changes, run `go build ./cmd/bwb` to verify. Run `go test ./...` after all changes.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/ui/modal/modal.go` (replace maxInt)
- `internal/ui/shared/issuerow/row.go` (replace maxInt/minInt)
- `internal/mode/details/model.go` (replace maxInt)
- `internal/app/model.go` (replace maxInt)
- `internal/gateway/beads/read_gateway.go` (replace containsString ONLY — filter arg dedup is in ia6)
- `internal/ui/loading/loading.go` (replace contains)
- `internal/ui/styles/colors.go` (consolidate normalizeIssueToken)
- `internal/config/keybindings.go` (extract mergeContextBindings helper)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero hand-rolled maxInt/minInt/contains helpers remain
- [ ] Single source for token normalization logic
- [ ] MergeKeyBindings uses extracted helper
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` succeeds
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] No behavioral changes
close_reason: Replaced duplicate max/min and contains helpers with builtins/slices.Contains, deduplicated keybinding merge logic, and documented token-normalization cycle constraint; build and tests pass.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Duplicate scope with ia6: instruction #4 (extract buildFilterArgs) is identical to ia6's first deliverable. Both tasks claim the same read_gateway.go refactor. Why: the tasker will do this work twice, or the second task will find it already done and be confused about scope. Suggested action: remove instruction #4 from 0jc entirely — ia6 already owns the gateway read method deduplication including buildFilterArgs.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Missing maxInt in details package: this task lists maxInt replacements in modal, issuerow, renderhelpers, and app — but misses internal/mode/details/model.go which also has a hand-rolled maxInt (line 142). Why: the goal is 'zero hand-rolled maxInt/minInt helpers remain' but this instance would survive. Suggested action: add internal/mode/details/model.go to the file list and replace maxInt with max().

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Inaccurate instruction #1: claims renderhelpers/helpers.go (~line 128) has maxInt/minInt to replace, but the file has NO maxInt or minInt definitions. Checked via grep — zero matches. Why: the tasker will look for code that doesn't exist and question the instructions. The actual maxInt locations are: modal/modal.go, issuerow/row.go, app/model.go, and mode/details/model.go (the last one already flagged as missing). Suggested action: remove renderhelpers from instruction #1's file list.

title: Remove all identified dead code (~200+ lines)
description:
## Description
Full codebase review identified substantial dead code across multiple packages. Removing it improves navigability and reduces maintenance burden without changing any behavior.

**NOTE:** This task is blocked by beads-workbench-xcp (overlay tests) because item 11 below removes overlay `BottomLeft`/`Top` position constants that xcp needs to test first. Only delete overlay position constants AFTER xcp confirms which are actually used by tests.

## Instructions
Remove the following dead code items:

### app package
1. `internal/app/model.go` — Delete `headerHelpLines()` function (discards args, returns nil)
2. `internal/app/model.go` — Delete `modeSwitchKey()` function (never called)
3. `internal/app/services.go` — Delete `SharedUIState` struct and all its allocations (allocated everywhere, read nowhere; toast state lives in `Model.toast`)

### config package
4. `internal/config/keybindings.go` — Delete `ModeBoardKey()`, `ModeSearchKey()`, `ModeDetailKey()`, `Label()` methods and the `keysByID` map (zero callers outside definition file)
5. `internal/config/load.go` — Inline or delete `errorsIsNotExist` wrapper (redundant since Go 1.16 — `os.IsNotExist` already calls `errors.Is`)
6. `internal/config/load.go` — Remove stale entries from `allowedSequenceMappingKeys` that reference unreachable keybinding nodes

### dashboard package
7. `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider.go` — Delete `actorResolver` field, `ActorResolver` interface, `EnvActorResolver` struct, and `NewBuiltInProviderWithActorResolver` constructor (stored but never read)
8. `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider_test.go` — Delete `TestBuiltInProviderDashboardsWithActorRemainsFourLaneDefault` (tests dead code, asserts same result as the non-actor test)

### ui packages
9. `internal/ui/styles/format.go` — Delete `FormatCommentIndicator()` (zero production callers)
10. `internal/ui/styles/format_test.go` — Delete the test for `FormatCommentIndicator`
11. `internal/ui/overlay/overlay.go` — Remove `BottomLeft` and `Top` **position constants** (iota values in the `Position` type) and their `case` branches in `calculatePosition`. These are iota constants, NOT standalone functions. Only remove them AFTER overlay tests (xcp) confirm they aren't needed for test coverage.
12. `internal/ui/styles/section.go` — Delete `TopLeftHint`, `BottomLeft`, `BottomRight` fields from `FormSectionConfig` and their rendering logic (~50 lines) — no caller sets these
13. `internal/ui/board/board.go` — Remove dead `availableWidth` variable (lines ~57-59, immediately shadowed)

### gateway package
14. `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go` — Remove `commandRunner()` nil-guard method (lines ~18-24, constructor prevents nil, read methods skip this guard — inconsistent)

### launcher/editor package
15. `internal/launcher/editor/service.go` — Remove `if e == nil` nil-receiver guard (line ~57-59, unreachable since constructor prevents nil)

After each removal, run `go build ./cmd/bwb` to verify compilation. Run `go test ./...` after all removals.

## Files to Modify
- `internal/app/model.go` (delete 2 functions)
- `internal/app/services.go` (delete SharedUIState)
- `internal/config/keybindings.go` (delete unused methods + map)
- `internal/config/load.go` (inline errorsIsNotExist, clean allowedSequenceMappingKeys)
- `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider.go` (delete actor resolver)
- `internal/dashboard/builtin_provider_test.go` (delete actor test)
- `internal/ui/styles/format.go` (delete FormatCommentIndicator)
- `internal/ui/styles/format_test.go` (delete its test)
- `internal/ui/overlay/overlay.go` (delete BottomLeft, Top position constants + case branches)
- `internal/ui/styles/section.go` (delete unused config fields + rendering)
- `internal/ui/board/board.go` (delete dead variable)
- `internal/gateway/beads/writes.go` (remove nil-guard method)
- `internal/launcher/editor/service.go` (remove nil-receiver guard)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All listed dead code items removed
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/bwb` succeeds
- [ ] `go vet ./...` clean
- [ ] `go test ./...` passes
- [ ] No behavioral changes — only dead code removed
close_reason: Removed all listed dead code across app/config/dashboard/ui/gateway/launcher; validated with incremental builds and final build+vet+test.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Inaccurate instruction #11: task says 'Delete BottomLeft() and Top() functions' but they are Position constants (iota values), not functions. The actual dead code is the BottomLeft and Top enum values plus their case branches in calculatePosition(). Why: a tasker following these instructions literally will look for functions that don't exist and get confused. Suggested action: reword to 'Remove unused BottomLeft and Top position constants from the iota block and their corresponding case branches in calculatePosition.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Scope risk: this is the riskiest task in the epic. It touches 13 files across 7 packages in a single task. A mistake in any one deletion breaks the build for all subsequent tasks. Why: a single monolithic dead-code-removal task means if the tasker misidentifies something as dead (e.g. SharedUIState.UI field is used in services.go constructor calls), the whole chain stalls. Suggested action: not splitting (that would be over-engineering), but the tasker should compile after EACH removal group (app, config, dashboard, ui, gateway, launcher) as the instructions already say. Emphasize this is mandatory, not optional — the acceptance criteria should include 'incremental build verification after each package group.'

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Potential false positive on SharedUIState: instruction #3 says 'Delete SharedUIState struct and all its allocations (allocated everywhere, read nowhere)'. I checked — SharedUIState is defined in services.go:13, has a UI field in Services struct (line 33), and is allocated in constructors (lines 68, 91). But the struct body needs checking — what fields does SharedUIState have, and are ANY of them read anywhere? If even one field is read transitively through services.UI.SomeField, deleting the whole struct is a breaking change. Why: 'allocated but never read' is a strong claim that needs verification at field level. Suggested action: tasker should grep for 'services.UI.' or '.UI.' references in app/ before deleting.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Correction to my previous SharedUIState concern: verified that services.UI (SharedUIState) is indeed never read — its only field ToastMessage appears only in the struct definition. The '.UI.' references in model.go are services.Config.UI (the config model), not services.UI (SharedUIState). The deletion is safe. No action needed on this point.

title: scenario key helpers are hardcoded — will drift from configurable keybindings
description:
internal/testing/ui/scenarios.go defines hardcoded key sequences (BoardToSearchKeys returns ctrl+space, OpenDetailKeys returns enter, etc.) that must match the keybinding configuration in internal/config. The project supports user-configurable keybindings (documented in CODING.md). These scenario helpers hardcode the defaults but have no connection to the actual default keybinding definitions.

If someone changes the default keybinding for open_detail from Enter to Space, these helpers silently become wrong and scenario tests pass for the wrong reason (they send keys that no longer match the bound action).

Suggested action: either (a) derive these from the default config keybinding definitions programmatically, or (b) add a comment at each helper documenting which config action it corresponds to, so a keybinding change has a clear audit trail to update.
close_reason: Noted — scenario helpers are test-only, acceptable for now

title: Dashboard: Query struct tagged-union pattern has no compile-time enforcement — wrong field can be populated for a given Type
description: In definition.go:41-46, Query uses a tagged-union pattern where Type selects which of ListIssues/ReadyIssues/BlockedIssues is populated. But there is zero enforcement — a caller can set Type=QueryTypeReadyIssues while populating ListIssues, and the consumer won't know until runtime. The built-in sections do this correctly today, but the pattern is fragile for future providers. Suggested action: at minimum, add a Validate() method on Query that checks exactly one payload matches Type. Or consider separate concrete query types behind an interface. The current approach works for 4 built-in sections but will not scale safely. File: internal/dashboard/definition.go:41-46
close_reason: Noted — low risk with 4 built-in sections, defer

title: FakeProcessRunner is unused outside its own test — consider removing or documenting intent
description: FakeProcessRunner in internal/testing/fakes/process_runner.go is never imported by any production test file. The launcher/service_test.go has its own local recordingRunner (a near-identical copy). Either: (a) the shared FakeProcessRunner was created speculatively and should be removed until needed, or (b) launcher/service_test.go should use it instead of the local copy. The current state means the fake exists, is tested, but provides no value — it's dead code. If it's intended for future use, add a comment stating that intent. If not, remove it and its test file.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-qyz (test helpers)

title: Duplicated hasGatewayCall helper across 3 test files
description: The same hasGatewayCall/hasGatewayMethod helper is copy-pasted in 3 test files: internal/app/model_test.go (line 1342), internal/mode/search/model_test.go (line 408), and internal/launcher/editor/service_test.go (line 110 as hasGatewayMethod). This is a natural fit for the fakes package: add a HasCall(calls []GatewayCall, method GatewayMethod) bool method or free function to internal/testing/fakes and remove the 3 local copies. The launcher/editor copy even uses a different name (hasGatewayMethod) which makes it harder to grep for usage patterns.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-qyz (test helpers)

title: Dashboard: DashboardDefinitionProvider interface has stuttering name — should be just Provider
description: In definition.go:10-12, the interface is named DashboardDefinitionProvider inside package dashboard. This produces the stuttered qualified name dashboard.DashboardDefinitionProvider at every call site (confirmed in internal/mode/board/model.go:40,59,356). Go naming convention (Effective Go, CodeReviewComments) says: avoid stuttering package name in exported identifiers. Should be dashboard.Provider or dashboard.DefinitionProvider. Downstream impact: 3 files reference the full name. This is a straightforward rename. File: internal/dashboard/definition.go:10-12
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-dsd (Go conventions)

title: FakeProcessRunner missing compile-time interface assertion
description: FakeProcessRunner in internal/testing/fakes/process_runner.go is the only fake that lacks a compile-time interface assertion (var _ launcher.ProcessRunner = (*FakeProcessRunner)(nil)). FakeBeadsGateway (line 101), FakeLauncher (line 25), and FakeEditor (line 24) all have these. If the ProcessRunner interface changes, FakeProcessRunner will silently drift and only fail when a consumer test happens to exercise the mismatch. Add the assertion for consistency and early breakage detection.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-qyz (test helpers)

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Trivial fix: add 'var _ launcher.ProcessRunner = (*FakeProcessRunner)(nil)' after the struct declaration at line 22 of process_runner.go. Also add the launcher import. Should take under 2 minutes.

title: Code review: modal.go Update passes non-key messages to textinput even when not focused
description: modal/modal.go:229-233 forwards any non-KeyMsg (including WindowSizeMsg which is handled above) to the focused text input's Update method. While WindowSizeMsg is caught first, any other future message types (custom msgs, tick msgs) will leak into textinput.Update when focusedInput >= 0. The textinput will silently ignore them now, but this is a latent coupling. Not critical but worth noting. Suggested action: consider guarding the textinput forwarding to only pass tea.KeyMsg types, making the intent explicit.
close_reason: Noted as minor latent coupling — defer

title: Code review: search_test.go golden helper has BWB_UPDATE_GOLDEN support that board_test.go and others lack
description: search/search_test.go:23-28 has BWB_UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 support for auto-updating golden files, but board/board_test.go and details/details_test.go do not. This is inconsistent: developers will expect golden update to work the same across all packages. The harness (testing/ui/harness.go) doesn't have this feature either, so search_test.go implemented it ad-hoc. Suggested action: either add BWB_UPDATE_GOLDEN support to the shared harness (AssertMatchesGoldenNormalized), or remove it from search_test.go to keep behavior consistent.
close_reason: Noted — inconsistency is minor, defer

title: Launcher TemplateInterpolator and DefinitionResolver are exported but only used internally
description:
TemplateInterpolator (service.go:88-106) and DefinitionResolver (service.go:56-86) are exported types, but neither is used outside the launcher package. DefinitionResolver is constructed and consumed entirely within NewService/launcherService. TemplateInterpolator is instantiated only in NewService.

Exporting these types suggests they are part of a public API contract when they are actually implementation details. This increases the API surface consumers must understand and constrains future refactoring.

Suggested fix: unexport both to `templateInterpolator` and `definitionResolver`. Keep `NewDefinitionResolver` exported only if there is an external consumer (there isn't currently). The `Definition` and `InterpolationContext` types are fine to stay exported since they are used in config wiring.

Files: internal/launcher/service.go, lines 56-106.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-dsd (Go conventions)

title: Code review: loading.contains should use slices.Contains from Go stdlib
description: loading/loading.go:70-76 implements a hand-rolled contains() function for string slice membership. Go 1.21+ provides slices.Contains in the standard library, and this project targets Go 1.24. Suggested action: replace the custom contains() with slices.Contains() and delete the helper.

The hint style and bottom title rendering can be re-added when there's a real user.

title: Dashboard: BuiltInProvider.actorResolver field is stored but never read — dead code

title: Code review: UI packages - ansiEscapePattern regexp duplicated in three test files
description: board/board_test.go:16, search/search_test.go:18, and issuerow/row_test.go:13 each declare identical 'var ansiEscapePattern = regexp.MustCompile(\x1b\[[0-9;]*m)'. The testing/ui/harness.go package already provides NormalizeOutput but no ANSI stripping helper. Additionally, board_test.go:18-35 defines its own assertGoldenNormalized that duplicates the harness's AssertMatchesGoldenNormalized plus ANSI stripping, while search_test.go:20-31 has yet another variant with BWB_UPDATE_GOLDEN support. title: Launcher service_test.go: recordingRunner duplicates FakeProcessRunner from shared fakes package
description:
service_test.go:11-31 defines a local `recordingRunner` that is functionally identical to `fakes.FakeProcessRunner` (same fields, same recording behavior, same defensive copy). The shared fakes package exists precisely for this purpose and is already documented in TESTING.md as the required test double.

This violates the project's 'shared deterministic seams live in internal/testing/fakes' convention. It also means any bug fix to the fake's recording behavior would not apply to these tests.

Suggested fix: replace all uses of the local `recordingRunner` in service_test.go with `fakes.FakeProcessRunner`, and change the test to `package launcher_test` (external test package, consistent with editor/service_test.go which already does this).

File: internal/launcher/service_test.go, lines 11-31.

title: Config: ModeBoardKey/ModeSearchKey/ModeDetailKey and keysByID map are dead code — zero callers
description: In keybindings.go:89, keysByID is populated in ResolveKeyBindings (lines 228-230) but only read by ModeBoardKey(), ModeSearchKey(), ModeDetailKey() (lines 291-301). A project-wide grep shows zero callers of these three methods outside the definition file itself. The Label() method (line 283) is also unused — no callers found. The keysByID map, its population logic, the three Mode*Key accessor methods, and the Label method are dead code. They add maintenance cost (keeping keysByID in sync with Shell bindings) for no benefit. Suggested action: remove keysByID, the three Mode*Key methods, and Label(). If needed later, Primary() already provides the same data without a separate cache. Files: internal/config/keybindings.go:89, 228-230, 283-301

title: Config: allowedSequenceMappingKeys has stale keybindings entries that duplicate allowedMappingKeys
description: In load.go:324-334, allowedSequenceMappingKeys includes entries for 'keybindings.shell' and 'keybindings.board' but NOT 'keybindings.search', 'keybindings.detail', or 'keybindings.modal'. Meanwhile, allowedMappingKeys (lines 317-321) already covers ALL five keybinding contexts. The YAML keybinding nodes are mapping nodes, not sequence nodes, so collectSequenceWarnings should never be invoked for keybinding contexts. The two keybinding entries in allowedSequenceMappingKeys are dead code that will never match, and the incomplete set (missing search/detail/modal) suggests copy-paste drift. Suggested action: remove the keybindings entries from allowedSequenceMappingKeys entirely. File: internal/config/load.go:332-334

title: Config: errorsIsNotExist wrapper is redundant — os.IsNotExist already handles os.ErrNotExist
description: In load.go:407-408, errorsIsNotExist checks both os.IsNotExist(err) and errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist). Since Go 1.16, os.IsNotExist wraps errors.Is(os.ErrNotExist) internally, making the second check redundant. The wrapper function itself can be replaced with errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) at the single call site (line 86), removing 3 lines and an unnecessary indirection. File: internal/config/load.go:407-408
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-2t4 (dead code removal)

title: Domain tests: missing edge cases for issue edit document — empty labels, cleared assignee, boundary priorities
description: issue_edit_document_test.go: The tests cover the happy-path round-trip and the no-change diff, but miss several important edge cases: (1) ClearLabels path in BuildIssueUpdateInput — when edited.Labels is empty but original has labels, ClearLabels should be true. This is untested. (2) Priority boundary values — only priority 2→1 is tested. Priority 0 and 4 parsing, and out-of-range values like 5 or -1, are not tested. (3) parseIssueEditPriority accepts 'P2' or 'p2' prefix syntax (line 275) but no test exercises this. (4) Empty assignee clearing — going from 'hans' to '' is untested in the diff. (5) Description with trailing/leading newlines — issueEditExtractField strips newlines (line 270) but description content newlines should be preserved. No test confirms multi-paragraph descriptions survive. Suggested action: add test cases for each of these 5 gaps.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-9uk (test improvements)

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Additional gap: TestParseIssueEditDocumentRejectsMissingMarkers (line 83-90) only tests a completely invalid document. It does not test partially valid documents — e.g. a document with the editable block but missing one field marker. This matters because a user could accidentally delete one field's markers while editing.

title: Gateway: DecodeJSONInto uses DisallowUnknownFields which will break when bd adds new fields
description: runner.go line 136: DecodeJSONInto calls decoder.DisallowUnknownFields(). This means if the bd CLI adds ANY new field to its JSON output, the gateway will return a decode error. For a TUI consuming an external CLI, this is backwards — you want to be tolerant of new fields and strict about fields you depend on. This will cause runtime breakage on bd version upgrades. Suggested action: remove DisallowUnknownFields() from DecodeJSONInto. The typed map[string]any decoding in read_gateway.go already handles field-by-field extraction safely.
close_reason: Covered by beads-workbench-ehq (strict JSON)

title: Gateway: replace hand-rolled containsString with slices.Contains from stdlib
description: read_gateway.go lines 433-441: containsString is a hand-rolled linear search for string-in-slice. Go 1.21+ provides slices.Contains which does the same thing. This project targets Go 1.24+. Using the stdlib version reduces code and signals intent more clearly. Suggested action: delete containsString, replace its 3 call sites with slices.Contains.

title: Gateway: massive code duplication in SearchIssues, searchIssuesFromList, and ListIssues arg building
description: read_gateway.go: SearchIssues (lines 219-256), searchIssuesFromList (lines 280-313), and ListIssues (lines 44-89) all independently build nearly identical CLI arg arrays for status/type/assignee/label/priority/limit filters. The searchIssuesFromList method is almost a copy-paste of SearchIssues with 'list' instead of 'search'. This violates DRY and means filter changes need updating in 3+ places. Suggested action: extract a shared buildFilterArgs(query) helper that appends the common filter flags, then have each method compose the subcommand name + shared filter args + method-specific args.
